expectations.text: Identifies Coverage as one of the two dimensions that dropped (from 95 to 70)
Identifies Evidence Quality as the other dropped dimension (from 100 to 60)
Provides at least 2 plausible causes for the Coverage drop (e.g. journey subdirs deleted, evidence purged, new features without new journeys)
expectations.evidence: Lines 19-23 list 4 causes: journey subdirs deleted, plans/ emptied, partial validation run, evidence directory relocation.

expectations.text: Provides at least 2 plausible causes for the Evidence Quality drop (e.g. empty/junk files, missing verdict file, files < 10 bytes)
expectations.evidence: Lines 31-34 list 3 causes: verdict writer never ran, empty evidence files, evidence filename pattern drift.

expectations.text: Recommends inspecting e2e-evidence/ for empty or zero-byte files
expectations.evidence: Line 43: 'find e2e-evidence -type f -size 0 and -size -11c — list empty/under-10-byte files'.

expectations.text: Recommends re-running validation to regenerate fresh evidence with all original journeys
expectations.evidence: Line 50: 'Re-run the full multi-platform validation sweep so all platform journeys repopulate e2e-evidence/<platform>/'.

expectations.text: Mentions the verdict / report.md file and the +30 bonus it unlocks on Evidence Quality
expectations.evidence: Line 27: '30 if a *VERDICT* or report.md exists'. Line 53: 'Require the verdict-writer phase to run and emit e2e-evidence/report.md every run — this single file returns +30 to Evidence Quality'.

expectations.text: Projects recovery to Grade A (aggregate ≥ 90) after the recommended remediation

claims.claim: Snapshot does not explain *why* artifacts disappear — only scoring formulas
claims.evidence: Line 59-61 explicitly calls this out as a limitation of the snapshot. Confirmed by direct read of snapshot/SKILL.md.

eval_feedback.overall: Snapshot answered equivalently — same pass rate as improved version. The assertions capture necessary-but-not-sufficient behaviors. Differentiation would require a harder prompt (e.g. one demanding the script be run).
